refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall maintain teleoperation link availability at or above the customer-defined target throughout the operational period.
refined_fr.rationale: Refining the original FR clarifies the performance metric (link availability) and ties it explicitly to the customer-defined target and operational timeframe, ensuring measurable and testable requirements.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure link uptime and downtime over the evaluation period using telemetry logs and compare against the customer-defined availability threshold.
sub_frs: FR1: Monitor teleoperation link status continuously.
FR2: Detect and report link failures promptly.
FR3: Recover or mitigate link failures to maintain availability.

candidate_dps: DP1: Implement redundant network links with automatic failover.
DP2: Real-time link health monitoring and alerting system.
DP3: Link failure recovery protocols and fallback modes.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1: Implement redundant network links with automatic failover.

potential_sources: Link monitoring tightly coupled with failover logic.
Recovery protocols depend on accurate failure detection.
coupling_check.notes: The selected DP logically supports the FR with manageable coupling; monitoring and recovery DPs can be modularized to reduce tight coupling.

assumptions_risks: Risk of increased system complexity impacting maintainability.
Risk that failover mechanisms may introduce latency affecting availability.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider integrating monitoring and recovery DPs in subsequent decomposition levels to address coupling and complexity.

im_boundary.name: Vehicle
im_boundary.scope: Physical network hardware and embedded failover logic within the vehicle systems.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Interface for redundant network connections enabling failover.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Provides link status data to higher-level monitoring modules.

module_containment_scores.notes: High containment as failover hardware and logic reside here; low diffusion risk.
Partial containment possible but would increase diffusion due to distributed logic.
Minimal containment; primarily monitors safety alerts, not link failover.
System-level coordination possible but less direct containment.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Failover logic and redundant network hardware are best contained within the Vehicle module to minimize diffusion and ensure tight control over physical network components.
containment_risks: Potential complexity in Vehicle module increasing integration effort.
Dependency on Vehicle hardware lifecycle for updates and maintenance.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Evaluate if some monitoring functions should be assigned to Teleoperation module to balance containment and diffusion.

fr_dp_gate.reason: Selected DP (redundant network links with failover) directly addresses the refined FR and supports measurable availability targets.

diffusion_risks: Some monitoring and alerting functions may diffuse across Vehicle and Teleoperation modules.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Failover logic is well contained in Vehicle; monitoring may require cross-module interfaces.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification via telemetry and network link status is feasible with current interfaces.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of failover DP to Vehicle module; monitoring and alerting to be clarified in next level.

recommended_revisions: Refine monitoring and recovery FRs and DPs in next decomposition level.
Clarify interface responsibilities between Vehicle and Teleoperation modules.

decision.justification: While the primary DP and IM assignment are sound, the monitoring and recovery aspects require further decomposition and clearer allocation to reduce diffusion risks and improve architectural clarity.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Monitor teleoperation link status continuously.
FR2: Detect and report link failures promptly.
FR3: Recover or mitigate link failures to maintain availability.

revise.why: To decompose monitoring and recovery functions explicitly, select corresponding DPs, and assign IM boundaries to reduce diffusion and improve modular ownership.

assumptions: Network infrastructure supports redundancy and failover.
Telemetry interface can provide sufficient link status data.
open_risks: Complexity of failover logic within Vehicle module.
Potential latency introduced by failover impacting availability.
evidence_needed: Telemetry data demonstrating link availability performance.
Test results of failover mechanisms under operational conditions.
